c_signature: mp_limb_t mpn_divrem_1(mp_limb_t *qp, mp_size_t qxn, mp_limb_t *np, mp_size_t nn, mp_limb_t d0)
doc:
Pure-TS port of GMP's mpn_divrem_1 -- divides a multi-precision integer by a single 64-bit limb, computing both quotient and remainder. Substrate-class -- operates on raw bigint arrays (decimal-string limbs over the wire), not src/core.ts MPFR values, so the schema ast_check requireCoreImport=false exemption applies.

C contract (GMP mpn_divrem_1 with qxn fractional quotient extension):
  Numerator: an (nn+qxn)-limb integer obtained by prepending qxn zero limbs to the LSB end of np[0..nn). I.e. np shifted left by qxn full limbs.
  Divisor: a single nonzero limb d0.
  Quotient: written to qp[0..nn+qxn), little-endian.
  Remainder: a single limb in [0, d0), returned as mp_limb_t.
  The remainder fits in one limb because the divisor is one limb.

Mini-gmp shim equivalence (mpfr/src/mpfr-mini-gmp.c L177-L213):
  The MPFR mini-gmp shim wraps the operation in mpz_init/mpz_tdiv_qr/mpz_clear -- semantically identical to the underlying BigInt truncated division `(np_int << (qxn * 64)) /qr d0`. The TS port uses native BigInt division and matches this semantics exactly.

TS signature (no-mutation-rewrite):
  mpn_divrem_1(qxn: number, np: readonly bigint[], nn: number, d0: bigint) -> { q: readonly bigint[], r: bigint }
  - qxn: number of zero limbs prepended to np to form the dividend (0 is the common case).
  - np: nn-limb dividend in little-endian limb order; each limb in [0, 2^64).
  - nn: length of np to consume; nn >= 0. (nn == 0 with qxn == 0 is degenerate -- empty dividend -- and returns {q: [], r: 0n}.)
  - d0: single nonzero divisor limb in (0, 2^64).
  - returns: q has length nn+qxn; r is a single bigint limb in [0, d0).

Class 'substrate' (50ms per-case budget). Tag minimums apply (happy>=20, edge>=30, adversarial>=10, fuzz>=50, mined>=5 or all available). Mined absent -- mpfr/tests/ has no isolatable mpn_divrem_1 test driver; it is exercised internally by mpfr_set_z / mpfr_get_str.
divergence_from_c:
1. C mutates qp in place and returns remainder as scalar; TS returns a fresh {q, r} object with q as a freshly-allocated readonly bigint[] and r as a bigint.
2. C accepts qxn > 0 with np providing the high-end nn limbs of a (nn+qxn)-limb dividend; TS port matches by computing dividend = np_combined << (qxn * 64) before division.
3. Validation: TS throws Error on nn < 0, qxn < 0, np.length < nn, d0 == 0n. C asserts d0 != 0 in normalized form.
4. Limb endianness: both little-endian (limbs[0] = least significant). Documented in spec, asserted at the limb-width invariant: GMP_NUMB_BITS == 64.
refs: GMP manual section 8.3 -- mpn_divrem_1 single-limb divisor variant.
mpfr/src/mpfr-mini-gmp.c L177-L213 -- the SHIM body the TS port mirrors (mpz_tdiv_qr semantics).
GMP section 8.3 limb endianness: limbs[0] is the least-significant 64-bit word.
CLAUDE.md 'Hallucination-risk callouts' -- GMP mpn limbs are LITTLE-ENDIAN by limb index.
